text.title: Outcome Based Roadmaps Advanced Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Outcome Based Roadmaps Advanced application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Roadmap MUST link outcomes across quarters via explicit edges; floating per-quarter outcomes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Cross-quarter chains expose dependency surprises early.

rule.statement: Every dependency edge MUST carry type {hard, soft} + rationale; un-typed edges are rejected.
Confidence MUST follow decay model (Q+1 high, Q+2 medium, Q+3+ low) unless cited evidence justifies higher; over-confident long-horizon items are rejected.
rule.rationale: Confidence-decay matches empirical accuracy; over-confidence rooms accumulate planning debt.

rule.statement: Roadmap MUST have one swim lane per product (≥2); single-lane roadmaps reduce to base methodology.
rule.rationale: Swim lanes prevent products competing inside one outcome.

rule.statement: Each quarter's review MUST update the multi-quarter chain (which edges shifted, which decayed); roadmap-without-review is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without iterative review the multi-quarter chain ossifies and stops representing reality.
